prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
Verily, as the harvest moon waxed full, the Lord Treasurer did proclaim a great "Annual Recurring Revenue," though no crops were sown nor sheaves gathered, but rather a host of phantoms within a silver casket did whisper promises of future gold.

Behold, the scribes speak in fevered tongues of "churn," a dread affliction not of the body, but of the subscriber, who doth vanish from the ledger as if spirited away by mischievous sprites, leaving naught but an empty seat at the feast.

They tally their conquests not in leagues of land or heads of cattle, but in "Net Dollar Retention," a most curious sorcery where a single coin placed in their vault at dawn becometh, by eventide, one and one-tenth of a coin, without the touch of alchemist or mint.

And lo, the grandest portent was a thing called "Free Cash Flow," a river of silver that appeareth from the very aether, which the court doth scrutinize with more fervor than the king's own astrologer reading the entrails of a fowl.
